title: New finding modules: P2-07 Real-User Field Experience, P2-08 Web Best Practices, P2-01 transparency INFO

files_forbidden: any component, route handler, or message catalog

acceptance_criteria: P2-07 (runRealUserFieldModule) consumes the parsed CrUX field data from LighthouseMetrics (shape from T-01). It emits a HIGH finding ONLY when URL-level loadingExperience.overall_category === 'SLOW' AND the lab score is >= 50. When only originLoadingExperience exists (no URL-level), it emits at most an INFO finding and NEVER a HIGH. When no field data at all is present it emits NO finding (no 'no data' noise).

P2-07 never emits a banner/HIGH from origin-level data; the module returns plain RawFinding[] and carries enough structure for the UI to render the mobile-only banner decision (the banner gating itself is enforced in the aggregator/UI tasks).

P2-08 (runBestPracticesModule) maps the failed best-practices audits parsed in T-01 into RawFinding[] with category 'Best Practices'; it is tolerant of an empty/absent best-practices set (returns []), and never throws on missing audits.

P2-01 core-web-vitals module additionally emits an INFO (not HIGH/penalty) finding when any single LAB Core Web Vital is in the Poor band, even if the overall blended score is decent — this is transparency only and must not change severity of existing findings.

All three modules follow the existing module signature/return conventions (async or sync per existing P2 pattern, RawFinding[]), correct moduleId strings ('P2-07','P2-08','P2-01'), and never invent findings, upgrade severity, or emit unredacted untrusted strings (length-cap any echoed audit/field text).

Unit tests for each module covering: P2-07 url-SLOW+lab>=50 -> HIGH, origin-only -> INFO max, absent -> none, url-SLOW but lab<50 -> not HIGH; P2-08 mapping + empty set; P2-01 single-Poor-CWV INFO. npm run lint and npm run test pass for touched code.
output_format: code plus a plain-English explanation of the P2-07 fire predicate and the P2-01 transparency rationale
